statement: Build only what the current requirement demands; defer everything else.
evidence_chunk: Adding a feature before it is needed costs twice: once to build it and once to refactor it when the real requirement arrives.

statement: Prefer the simplest design that solves the problem; complexity is a liability.
evidence_chunk: Every layer of abstraction added speculatively must be maintained, understood, and debugged without the benefit of a concrete use case to guide it.

statement: Every piece of knowledge must have a single, authoritative representation in the system.
evidence_chunk: Duplication of logic or data leads to divergence: when you change one copy, the others silently drift, producing contradictions the compiler cannot catch.

statement: Make behavior visible in code; implicit magic is technical debt.
evidence_chunk: Code that relies on hidden conventions requires the reader to hold a mental model of side-effects that are not visible at the call site.

statement: Detect and report errors at the earliest possible point rather than continuing with invalid state.
evidence_chunk: Systems that fail fast surface bugs at the point of introduction; systems that fail slow propagate corruption through the call stack, making root cause analysis expensive.

statement: Validate all inputs at system boundaries; trust nothing from outside your module.
evidence_chunk: Defensive programming assumes that callers will pass incorrect data; validation at boundaries prevents errors from propagating deep into the system where their origin is obscured.

statement: Assume components will fail and design explicit recovery paths for each failure mode.
evidence_chunk: In distributed systems, partial failure is the norm. A service without explicit failure handling treats all failures as catastrophic, preventing graceful degradation.

statement: Write a failing test before writing the code that makes it pass.
evidence_chunk: Writing the test first forces you to define the interface before the implementation, producing APIs shaped by the caller rather than by the implementer.

statement: Each test must set up its own state and tear it down, with no shared mutable state across tests.
evidence_chunk: Tests that share state create order dependencies: a test that passes in isolation may fail when run after another, making the suite non-deterministic.

statement: Mock only at system boundaries such as databases and HTTP; never mock internals you own.

citation.book: Growing Object-Oriented Software Guided by Tests

evidence_chunk: Mocking internal collaborators creates tests that verify implementation detail rather than behavior, making any refactoring that preserves behavior appear to break tests.

statement: Each module or class should have exactly one reason to change.
evidence_chunk: A module with two responsibilities will be changed by two different stakeholders; their independent edits will interfere, creating unexpected coupling.

statement: High-level modules must not depend on low-level modules; both should depend on abstractions.
evidence_chunk: Direct dependency on concrete implementations couples modules at the source level, making it impossible to substitute implementations without modifying callers.

statement: Keep functions, classes, and modules small enough to be understood in a single reading.
evidence_chunk: Small units can be named precisely, tested in isolation, and understood without requiring context from surrounding code; large units accumulate hidden responsibilities.

statement: Emit structured logs, metrics, and traces from the start; you cannot debug what you cannot observe.
evidence_chunk: Systems without observability force engineers to reproduce failures locally; with observability you can understand any system state from production telemetry alone.

statement: When a deployment causes an incident, roll back to the last known-good state before debugging.
evidence_chunk: Fix-forward prolongs outages because debugging under time pressure in a degraded system produces unreliable conclusions; rollback restores service and gives a clean environment.
